name: job_description_agent
description: Parses free-text Job Descriptions (in English or Spanish, well or poorly written, formal or informal) and returns a normalized JSON structure with the role title, a concise description, the management level, and a cleaned list of required skills. It is the mandatory first step of the recruiting flow before searching for internal candidates.
when_to_use: When the user provides a raw job description text and wants it parsed, structured, or normalized — or before passing a JD to busquedas_internas_agent. Always use this agent first when the input is free-text.

actions.name: parsear_jd
actions.description: Receives a free-text Job Description and extracts four structured fields: the formal role title, a 3-4 sentence role summary, the management level (from a fixed enum), and a normalized list of up to 15 skills. Does not search candidates, call the ATS, or store any data.

request_schema.jd_texto: string — the full raw Job Description text, in any format or language

conventions.management_levels: Output is always exactly one of: 'Individual Contributor', 'Team Lead', 'Manager', 'Senior Manager', 'Director', 'Vice President'. When ambiguous, the agent picks the lower level.
conventions.skills_format: PascalCase for technologies and tools (e.g., 'Python', 'AWS', 'PostgreSQL'); lowercase in Spanish for soft skills (e.g., 'liderazgo', 'comunicación efectiva'). Duplicates are removed. Maximum 15 skills returned, prioritizing those explicitly mentioned in the text.
conventions.role_title: Uses the most formal and descriptive variant of the title found in the JD. Never shortens or paraphrases.
conventions.state: Stateless: single-request parsing with no side effects. No data is written or stored.
